programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: (PG) Jerry has dinner with his parents and Uncle Leo. Elaine uses Kramer as her boy friend in trying to fool her shrink and Jerry tries to buy back the watch.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A stand-up comedian navigates everyday life in New York with his eccentric group of friends, dealing with trivial yet absurd situations. Their conversations and misadventures revolve around social awkwardness, relationships, and the minutiae of daily life. Through ironic twists and self-centered decisions, their actions often lead to humorous and unexpected consequences.

programme.title: Seinfield: Episode
programme.desc: A stand-up comedian navigates everyday life in New York with his eccentric group of friends, dealing with trivial yet absurd situations. Their conversations and misadventures revolve around social awkwardness, relationships, and the minutiae of daily life. Through ironic twists and self-centered decisions, their actions often lead to humorous and unexpected consequences.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: (PG) Jerry takes pity on a poor foreigner whose immigration papers were mistakenly left in his unopened mailbox.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: A stand-up comedian navigates everyday life in New York with his eccentric group of friends, dealing with trivial yet absurd situations. Their conversations and misadventures revolve around social awkwardness, relationships, and the minutiae of daily life. programme.desc: A stand-up comedian navigates everyday life in New York with his eccentric group of friends, dealing with trivial yet absurd situations. Their conversations and misadventures revolve around social awkwardness, relationships, and the minutiae of daily life. Through ironic twists and self-centered decisions, their actions often lead to humorous and unexpected consequences.
